features.summary: Facebook reported Q3 2021 revenue of $29 billion, up 35% year-over-year, with ad revenue of $28.3 billion. Daily active users reached 1.93 billion, growing 6% annually. The company faced significant headwinds from Apple's iOS 14 changes affecting ad targeting and measurement, along with macroeconomic factors including supply chain issues and slowing e-commerce growth. Facebook announced plans to separate Facebook Reality Labs as a distinct reporting segment starting Q4, with expected $10 billion investment impact on operating profit in 2021.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q4 2021 revenue between $31.5-34 billion, reflecting continued uncertainty from iOS 14 changes and macro factors. For 2022, total expenses are projected at $91-97 billion (29-38% growth) driven by technical hiring and infrastructure investments. Capital expenditures are expected to reach $29-34 billion in 2022, primarily for AI/machine learning capabilities to improve ad targeting and content ranking. The company is pivoting toward serving younger adults (18-29) and expects Reality Labs investments to grow significantly over the next several years.
features.risks: Apple's iOS 14 changes created the largest headwind, reducing ad targeting accuracy and measurement capabilities, with full impact expected to continue into 2022. Supply chain disruptions and labor shortages are reducing advertiser demand, while slowing e-commerce growth from pandemic normalization affects revenue. Increased competition from TikTok and other platforms threatens user engagement, particularly among younger demographics. Regulatory scrutiny and potential restrictions on transatlantic data transfers pose additional operational risks.
features.key_drivers: Reels is becoming the primary engagement driver across platforms, with management expecting it to match Stories' importance. The company is making substantial investments in AI and machine learning to rebuild targeting systems with less user data and improve content recommendations. Commerce tools and on-platform shopping are being developed to help businesses navigate iOS changes. The metaverse initiative through Reality Labs represents a major long-term growth bet, with goals to reach one billion users and hundreds of billions in digital commerce by decade's end.
